sections.content:
The block is still one big white block (100 pixels). Clicking on it doesn't do anything. So clicking on game objects doesn't work.
Let me reconsider the problem. The only things that change the game area are:
1. Selecting a color from the toolbar (changes agent color)
2. SPACE (projects agent color onto block below)
SPACE with purple → splits block into purple top + white bottom
SPACE with white → makes entire block white
The goal panel shows white top + purple bottom. I can't achieve this directly.
Wait, maybe I'm wrong about what SPACE does. Let me think about what happens when the block is already split. Currently we have purple top + white bottom. What if I SPACE with white when the block is split? Does it paint the top block (which is adjacent to the agent) or does it merge and then paint?
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the goal is already achieved and I just need to confirm it. Let me look at the panel again. The top-left panel shows white on top and purple on bottom. The current game area has purple on top and white on bottom. These are opposite!
Wait, actually let me re-examine. Maybe the game is about matching the panel pattern. But I can't create white-top/purple-bottom with SPACE. Unless...
What if SPACE works differently when the block below is already split? Let me try:
1. Current state: purple top, white bottom
2. Select white, SPACE → does it paint just the top block (making it white), leaving bottom as white too?
Let me try this.
